description: Tropical shrub to 5m producing bright red cherry-like fruits extremely high in Vitamin C (50-100x orange). Fruits within 2-3 years. Needs frost-free position. Beautiful pink flowers.
Tropical shrub to 6m producing red seed pods yielding annatto - natural food coloring used worldwide. Pink flowers. Seeds also medicinal. Frost sensitive.
Succulent perennial with thick fleshy leaves containing medicinal gel. Drought tolerant, prefers well-drained soil. Used topically for burns and skin care.
Dual-purpose plant - leaves eaten as greens, seeds as grain. Chinese Spinach varieties grown for leaves. Heat loving and drought tolerant. Self-seeds readily.
Dual-purpose plant - leaves eaten as greens, seeds as grain. Chinese Spinach varieties grown for leaves. Heat loving and drought tolerant. Self-seeds readily.
Indian Gooseberry tree to 10m. Fruits extremely high in Vitamin C - sacred in Ayurvedic medicine. Deciduous. Drought tolerant once established. Frost sensitive when young.
Deciduous fruit tree. Seedlings variable - may take 5-10 years to fruit. Good for wildlife and biomass in syntropic systems.

Deciduous stone fruit tree, 4-8m tall. Early flowering, produces sweet orange fruit in summer. Varieties like Bentley suited to warmer climates. In syntropic systems occupies high strata with seasonal canopy. Requires good drainage and benefits from wind protection.

Edible canna producing large starchy rhizomes. Fast-growing to 2m with banana-like leaves and red flowers. Rhizomes used as potato substitute or processed into gluten-free arrowroot flour. Leaves edible and useful as mulch or animal fodder. Frost tender, dies back in winter.
Native Australian twining climber with blue bell-shaped flowers. Purple edible berry fruit. Frost tolerant groundcover or climber.
Subtropical fruit tree with rich creamy flesh. Requires well-drained soil - will not tolerate wet feet. Plant A and B types together for better pollination and heavier crops.
Subtropical fruit tree with rich creamy flesh. Requires well-drained soil - will not tolerate wet feet. Plant A and B types together for better pollination and heavier crops.
Perennial tropical grass producing massive biomass for mulching. Also known as elephant grass or napier grass. Grows 3-4m tall. Excellent for erosion control and chop-and-drop.

Fast-growing tropical fruit producing bunches within 12-18 months. Excellent biomass - stems contain 90% water, perfect for chop-and-drop. Pioneer species providing quick shade. Remove suckers to maintain productivity.
Annual cereal grass used as green manure and cover crop in syntropic systems. Fast biomass producer that improves soil structure. Chop-and-drop before seeding to return nutrients. Cold tolerant.
Annual cereal grass used as green manure and cover crop in syntropic systems. Fast biomass producer that improves soil structure. Chop-and-drop before seeding to return nutrients. Cold tolerant.
Thai basil variety with distinctive anise-like flavor and purple stems. Essential for Southeast Asian cuisine. Loves heat.

Greek varietyCompact, bushy perennial basil forming a dense mound of tiny aromatic leaves. More cold-tolerant than sweet basil. In syntropic systems works as low-to-medium strata aromatic companion plant. Repels pests and attracts beneficial insects. Can persist for multiple seasons in frost-free areas.
Thai basil variety with distinctive anise-like flavor and purple stems. Essential for Southeast Asian cuisine. Loves heat.
Classic sweet Genovese-type basil. Essential for pesto and Italian cooking. Pinch flowers to prolong harvest.
Mixed variety basil seeds producing range of colors and flavors. Annual herb - succession plant through warm months.
Climbing bean varieties needing support structure. Higher yielding than bush types. Includes Blue Lake and Snake Bean varieties. Keep picked for continuous production.
Climbing bean varieties needing support structure. Higher yielding than bush types. Includes Blue Lake and Snake Bean varieties. Keep picked for continuous production.
Climbing bean varieties needing support structure. Higher yielding than bush types. Includes Blue Lake and Snake Bean varieties. Keep picked for continuous production.
Bush bean varieties not requiring support. Fast growing nitrogen-fixer. Many varieties including Purple Beauty, Brown Beauty, Borlotti. Pick frequently to extend harvest.
Bush bean varieties not requiring support. Fast growing nitrogen-fixer. Many varieties including Purple Beauty, Brown Beauty, Borlotti. Pick frequently to extend harvest.
Mixed flower seed blend specifically selected to attract bees and butterflies. Contains nectar and pollen-rich flowers.
Root vegetable with edible leaves and sweet red roots. Easy to grow year-round in mild climates. Related to silverbeet. Thin seedlings as multiple seeds per cluster.
Root vegetable with edible leaves and sweet red roots. Easy to grow year-round in mild climates. Related to silverbeet. Thin seedlings as multiple seeds per cluster.
Root vegetable with edible leaves and sweet red roots. Easy to grow year-round in mild climates. Related to silverbeet. Thin seedlings as multiple seeds per cluster.
Native Australian nitrogen-fixing climbing vine. Striking black and yellow pea flowers. Excellent for covering fences and trellises.

Australian native nitrogen-fixing tree to 8m. Excellent pioneer for poor soils. Fixes nitrogen via Frankia bacteria. Fine needle-like foliage provides light shade. Fire-resistant. Important habitat for Black Cockatoos.
Perennial berry bramble producing dark sweet fruit. Thorny canes need support. Very vigorous - requires management to prevent spread. Fruits on second-year wood.
Acid-loving berry shrub requiring pH 4.5-5.5. Needs consistent moisture and good drainage. Beautiful autumn foliage.
Blue-flowered annual herb attracting bees. Cucumber-flavored leaves and flowers are edible. Self-seeds readily. Excellent companion plant.
Blue-flowered annual herb attracting bees. Cucumber-flavored leaves and flowers are edible. Self-seeds readily. Excellent companion plant.
White-flowered annual herb attracting bees. Cucumber-flavored leaves and flowers are edible. Self-seeds readily. Excellent companion plant.

Cool season nitrogen-fixing legume producing large edible beans. Excellent green manure and cover crop. Deep roots improve soil structure. Frost tolerant. Part of traditional green manure mixes in syntropic systems.

Cool season nitrogen-fixing legume producing large edible beans. Excellent green manure and cover crop. Deep roots improve soil structure. Frost tolerant. Part of traditional green manure mixes in syntropic systems.
Cool season vegetable. Harvest main head then side shoots continue producing. Rich in vitamins and minerals.
Cool season vegetable. Harvest main head then side shoots continue producing. Rich in vitamins and minerals.
Cool season vegetable. Harvest main head then side shoots continue producing. Rich in vitamins and minerals.
Cool season brassica producing small cabbage-like sprouts along tall stem. Needs long cool growing season. Flavor sweetens after frost. Remove lower leaves as sprouts develop.
Fast-growing green manure ready to slash in 6-8 weeks. Excellent bee forage with white flowers. Accumulates phosphorus. Seeds edible. Not a true wheat - gluten free.

Araucaria bidwillii, commonly known as the bunya pine, banya or bunya-bunya, is a large evergreen coniferous tree in the family Araucariaceae which is endemic to Australia. Its natural range is southeast Queensland with two very small, disjunct populations in northeast Queensland's World Heritage listed Wet Tropics.
Classic Australian grey pumpkin with dense orange flesh. Excellent keeper - stores for months. Sweet nutty flavor.
Crinkled leaf savoy cabbage with mild sweet flavor. More tender than smooth-leaf varieties. Frost tolerant.
Compact round green cabbage. Early maturing variety good for small spaces. Firm crisp heads.
Red cabbage variety rich in anthocyanins. Good for salads and pickling. Stores well.
Crinkled leaf savoy cabbage with mild sweet flavor. More tender than smooth-leaf varieties. Frost tolerant.
Medicinal flower with edible petals. Attracts beneficial insects and pollinators. Self-seeds readily.
Perennial fruiting plant producing golden berries in papery husks. Sweet-tart flavor. Self-seeds readily. Frost sensitive but regrows from roots.
Sweet pepper variety. Needs warm conditions and regular water. Fruits green ripening to red, yellow or orange depending on variety.
Red sweet capsicum. Allow to fully ripen on plant for sweetest flavor and highest vitamin C content.
Nitrogen-fixing tree producing edible pods as chocolate substitute. Drought tolerant once established, excellent for syntropic systems providing shade and biomass.
Root vegetable rich in beta-carotene. Sow direct as dislikes transplanting. Thin seedlings for proper root development. Loose deep soil produces best roots.
Fast-growing tropical shrub to 4m with large palmate leaves. Seeds produce castor oil. CAUTION: Seeds highly toxic. Excellent biomass pioneer - grows rapidly on poor soils. Self-seeds readily.
Prominent wetland plant with robust grass-like appearance and cigar-shaped brown flower spikes. Excellent for water filtration and erosion control in dam edges.
Cool season brassica grown for white curd. More demanding than other brassicas. Needs consistent moisture and nutrients. Tie leaves over curd to keep white.
Biennial vegetable grown for crisp stalks. Heavy feeder requiring rich moist soil. Harvest outer stalks first or whole plant.
Heirloom self-blanching celery with yellow-green stalks. No earthing up required. Thick ribs, crisp texture, mild flavor without bitterness. Dwarf compact habit to 40-60cm.

Pascal-type heirloom celery introduced 1969. Dark green stalks 11-12 inches to first joint. Smooth, thick, non-fibrous with crisp juicy texture and subtle nutty flavor. Large flavorful leaves edible for salads and soups.
Low-growing aromatic herb with daisy-like flowers. Used for calming herbal tea. Apple-scented foliage. Perennial groundcover type.
Medium-hot jalapeño pepper. Thick-walled fruits good fresh, pickled or smoked (chipotle). Heavy producer.
Medium-hot jalapeño pepper. Thick-walled fruits good fresh, pickled or smoked (chipotle). Heavy producer.

Compact, prolific chilli variety producing small, intensely hot fruits. Also known as Thai chilli. Plants are bushy and productive over multiple seasons in frost-free areas. Excellent companion plant attracting beneficial insects when flowering. Fruits ripen from green to red.
Hot cayenne pepper variety. Long thin red fruits excellent for drying. Prolific producer in warm conditions.

Bushy hot chilli producing a colourful mix of long slender peppers ripening yellow, green and red. Very hot. Compact habit suits containers and dense syntropic understory plantings. Prolific producer in warm conditions.
Medium-hot jalapeño pepper. Thick-walled fruits good fresh, pickled or smoked (chipotle). Heavy producer.
Perennial onion-flavored herb forming clumps. Pink-purple flowers edible. Cut regularly to encourage fresh growth.

Perennial herb with flat leaves and garlic-flavoured taste. Grows in clumps, spreading slowly. White star-shaped flowers attract pollinators. In syntropic systems serves as low-strata edible companion plant with pest-repelling properties. Hardy and low-maintenance once established.
Vigorous perennial vine with edible fruits, shoots and tubers. Single plant can produce 100+ fruit. Needs strong support structure. Frost sensitive tops but perennial root survives mild frost.

Cold-hardy citrus tree to 4m producing highly aromatic yellow fruit prized for zest and juice. More frost tolerant than most citrus. Thorny. Slow growing but long-lived. Used extensively in Japanese cuisine.

Low-growing perennial legume, spreading via stolons. Excellent nitrogen fixer and living mulch. In syntropic systems serves as ground-cover layer in placenta/low strata, fixing atmospheric nitrogen and suppressing weeds. Attracts pollinators. Tolerates mowing and foot traffic.
Perennial nitrogen-fixing legume. Excellent bee forage with pink flowers. Living mulch and green manure. Deep roots bring up minerals. Edible flowers.
Perennial nitrogen-fixing legume. Excellent bee forage with pink flowers. Living mulch and green manure. Deep roots bring up minerals. Edible flowers.

Low-growing perennial legume, spreading via stolons. Excellent nitrogen fixer and living mulch. In syntropic systems serves as ground-cover layer in placenta/low strata, fixing atmospheric nitrogen and suppressing weeds. Attracts pollinators. Tolerates mowing and foot traffic.
Tropical understory shrub to 5m producing coffee beans. Prefers filtered light - ideal below taller trees. First harvest in 3-4 years. White fragrant flowers. Frost sensitive.
Tropical understory shrub to 5m producing coffee beans. Prefers filtered light - ideal below taller trees. First harvest in 3-4 years. White fragrant flowers. Frost sensitive.
Deep-rooted nutrient accumulator bringing minerals from subsoil. Excellent for chop-and-drop mulching - produces massive biomass. Cut 4-5 times per season.
Aromatic herb - leaves (cilantro) and seeds both used. Bolts quickly in heat - plant in cool season or part shade. Self-seeds readily. Attracts beneficial insects.
Warm season grass producing sweet corn cobs. Manning Pride is NSW-adapted variety. Plant in blocks for wind pollination. Excellent biomass from stalks after harvest.
Warm season grass producing sweet corn cobs. Manning Pride is NSW-adapted variety. Plant in blocks for wind pollination. Excellent biomass from stalks after harvest.
Warm season grass producing sweet corn cobs. Manning Pride is NSW-adapted variety. Plant in blocks for wind pollination. Excellent biomass from stalks after harvest.
Mixed flower seed blend for cottage garden style plantings. Contains various annual and perennial flowers. Species composition varies by supplier.
Tropical nitrogen-fixing legume. Excellent summer green manure. Drought tolerant. Edible pods and dried peas. Varieties include Black-Eyed Pea.
Tropical nitrogen-fixing legume. Excellent summer green manure. Drought tolerant. Edible pods and dried peas. Varieties include Black-Eyed Pea.
Long burpless cucumber variety. Mild flavor without bitterness. Needs warm soil and consistent moisture.
Long burpless cucumber variety. Mild flavor without bitterness. Needs warm soil and consistent moisture.
Short Lebanese-style cucumber. Thin skin, crisp flesh, mild flavor. Pick young for best quality.
Long burpless cucumber variety. Mild flavor without bitterness. Needs warm soil and consistent moisture.

Small evergreen tree, 2-4m tall. Aromatic leaves essential in Indian cuisine. Frost-sensitive but thrives in subtropical NSW. In syntropic systems works as medium-strata aromatic and culinary tree. Attracts beneficial insects when flowering. Slow to establish but productive once mature.

Native Australian rainforest tree growing 5-10m. Produces large dark purple plums directly from trunk (cauliflorous). Fruit is tart and rich in antioxidants, used in jams, sauces and bush tucker cuisine. Needs sheltered position with some shade when young. Frost-sensitive but suits subtropical NSW. Valuable native food tree in syntropic systems.

Australian native flax lily with tough strap-like leaves and small blue-purple flowers followed by purple berries. Hardy groundcover tolerant of drought and frost. Provides native habitat, erosion control and food for birds.
Aromatic herb with feathery leaves. Seeds and leaves both culinary. Attracts beneficial insects. Self-seeds readily.
Long purple eggplant variety. Needs warm conditions and consistent moisture. Harvest when skin is glossy.
Long purple eggplant variety. Needs warm conditions and consistent moisture. Harvest when skin is glossy.
Native eucalypt
Native Australian paper daisy. Pink and white flowers excellent for drying. Annual wildflower. Drought tolerant.
Aromatic herb with anise-flavored bulb, fronds and seeds. Florence fennel grown for bulb. Attracts beneficial insects. Self-seeds readily.

Deciduous fruit tree producing sweet fruits. Drought tolerant once established. Loves warm Mediterranean-type climate but produces well in subtropical and temperate areas. Give sunny position and well-drained soil.
source: Daleys Fruit Nursery (bought plant), FFC (1st ORCHARD)

description: Australian native rainforest citrus producing unique caviar-like lime pearls. Thorny shrub to 6m. Fruit highly prized by chefs. Slow growing but very long-lived. Prefers part shade when young.
Tall Australian native eucalypt growing to 50m. Excellent honey producer and timber tree. Fast-growing emergent providing wildlife habitat and wind protection.
Delicate blue flowering plant. Biennial or short-lived perennial. Self-seeds freely. Excellent for cottage gardens and attracting pollinators.

Pungent bulb crop grown for culinary and medicinal use. Plant cloves in autumn for summer harvest. Excellent companion plant with pest-repelling properties. Requires vernalisation (cold period) for bulb formation. In syntropic systems, works as low-strata ground layer providing pest deterrence.

Perennial herb with flat leaves and garlic-flavoured taste. Grows in clumps, spreading slowly. White star-shaped flowers attract pollinators. In syntropic systems serves as low-strata edible companion plant with pest-repelling properties. Hardy and low-maintenance once established.
Scented leaf geranium with rose-like fragrance. Used for aromatics and pest management. Drought tolerant.
Tropical rhizome crop. Plant in spring, harvest when leaves yellow in autumn. Needs warmth, moisture and rich soil.
Tropical rhizome related to ginger with distinct pine-citrus flavor. Essential for Thai cooking. Needs warmth and humidity.
French-American red wine grape variety. Disease resistant and productive. Deep colored wine. Needs trellising. Prefers warm dry autumn for ripening.
French-American red wine grape variety. Disease resistant and productive. Deep colored wine. Needs trellising. Prefers warm dry autumn for ripening.

Large evergreen citrus tree, 5-10m tall. Produces large tart-sweet fruit. Vigorous grower needing more space than other citrus. In syntropic systems occupies high strata as productive canopy tree. Prefers warm, frost-free conditions with consistent moisture.
Mixed seed blend for cover cropping and green manure. May include legumes, grasses, and brassicas for soil building.
Drought tolerant once established. Can become weedy - harvest all fruit.
Drought tolerant once established. Can become weedy - harvest all fruit.
Drought tolerant once established. Can become weedy - harvest all fruit.
Small fruiting tree with sweet yellow fruit. Drought tolerant once established. Can become weedy - harvest all fruit.
Drought tolerant once established. Can become weedy - harvest all fruit.
Aromatic native eucalypt with peppermint-scented leaves. Source of eucalyptus oil. Tolerates frost and poor soils.

Aromatic semi-evergreen herb to 60cm with blue-purple flower spikes. Excellent bee plant and companion plant. Traditionally used medicinally. Drought tolerant once established. Attracts beneficial insects.
Fast-growing nitrogen-fixing tree with edible cotton-like fruit pulp. Excellent shade tree and biomass producer. Pods contain sweet white edible flesh around seeds.
Unusual Brazilian fruit tree with grapes growing directly on trunk (cauliflory). Sweet grape-like flavor. Slow growing but long-lived. Multiple fruiting flushes per year once mature.
Ornamental tree with spectacular purple flowers in spring. Provides light dappled shade. Deciduous in winter.
Tropical tree producing world's largest tree fruit - up to 35kg. Sweet flesh when ripe, savory meat substitute when unripe. Related to breadfruit. Needs frost-free tropical conditions.
Nero di Toscana or Lacinato kale with dark blue-green strap leaves. Heat tolerant dinosaur kale. Highly nutritious cut-and-come-again leafy green.
Native Australian perennial with tubular flowers resembling kangaroo paws. Attracts birds. Drought tolerant once established.
Native Australian nitrogen-fixing climbing vine. Striking black and yellow pea flowers. Excellent for covering fences and trellises. Same species as Black Coral Pea.

Small evergreen citrus tree, 2-4m tall. Produces small oval fruit eaten whole including skin. More cold-hardy than most citrus. In syntropic systems works as medium-strata productive tree. Compact habit makes it suitable for tighter spacing.
Fragrant Mediterranean shrub with purple flower spikes. Drought tolerant. Attracts bees and beneficial insects. Prune after flowering.
Fragrant Mediterranean shrub with purple flower spikes. Drought tolerant. Attracts bees and beneficial insects. Prune after flowering.
Fragrant Mediterranean shrub with purple flower spikes. Drought tolerant. Attracts bees and beneficial insects. Prune after flowering.
Hardy onion relative grown for white stem. Hill soil around stems to blanch. Long growing season.
Hardy onion relative grown for white stem. Hill soil around stems to blanch. Long growing season.
Citrus fruit tree producing year-round in subtropical conditions. Needs regular water and feeding. Frost sensitive when young.
Perennial herb with lemon-scented leaves. Used for tea and culinary. Spreads readily - can become invasive. Attracts bees. Calming medicinal properties.
Australian native rainforest tree with intensely lemon-scented leaves. Leaves used in cooking and for essential oil. Beautiful cream flowers attract bees.

Australian native tea tree (5–8m) with strongly lemon-scented foliage. Tolerates drought once established. Attracts native bees and butterflies. Used for essential oil, hedge planting, and ornamental landscaping. Distinct from Tea Tree Oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) — same family, different genus.
Aromatic tropical grass with lemony stalks. Essential for Asian cooking. Forms large clumps. Frost sensitive.
Succession plant for continuous supply.
Upright cos/romaine lettuce with crisp leaves. Heat tolerant variety. Harvest outer leaves or whole head.
Mixed salad lettuce varieties for cut-and-come-again harvesting. Succession plant for continuous supply.
Native Australian rainforest tree with edible pink/purple fruit. Dense glossy foliage. Good hedging plant. Attracts birds. Species to be verified.
Flax grown for nutritious oilseeds. Blue flowers. Seeds rich in omega-3 fatty acids. Ancient crop also used for fiber.
Brown-seeded flax variety grown for nutritious oilseeds rich in omega-3 fatty acids. Also used as green manure cover crop. Blue flowers.
American sweetgum. Large deciduous tree with spectacular autumn color. Star-shaped leaves. Spiky seed balls.
Tropical fruit tree related to lychee. Translucent sweet flesh around single seed. More cold tolerant than lychee. Dense evergreen canopy. Dragon's Eye fruit.
Climbing vine producing edible fruit when young. Mature fruits dry to produce natural bath sponges. Needs long warm growing season.
Ornamental annual lupin with blue/pink/white flowers. Nitrogen-fixing legume. Good for cut flowers and soil building.

Australian native rainforest tree producing premium edible nuts. World's most valuable commercial nut crop originated from NSW. Grows 10-15m. Needs well-drained acid soil. Beautiful cream flower racemes.
Tropical tree to 18m with edible chestnut-like seeds. Often sold as money tree houseplant. Seeds roasted taste like peanuts. Tolerates wet conditions.
Tropical fruit tree producing delicious sweet fruit. Long-lived climax species. Grafted trees fruit quicker than seedlings. Needs frost-free position.
Companion plant repelling pests with strong scent. Root exudates suppress soil nematodes. Orange and yellow flowers attract pollinators. Easy annual.
Companion plant repelling pests with strong scent. Root exudates suppress soil nematodes. Orange and yellow flowers attract pollinators. Easy annual.
Companion plant repelling pests with strong scent. Root exudates suppress soil nematodes. Orange and yellow flowers attract pollinators. Easy annual.
Companion plant repelling pests with strong scent. Root exudates suppress soil nematodes. Orange and yellow flowers attract pollinators. Easy annual.
Native Australian dense shrub to 4m with ball-shaped cream flowers. Highly attractive to native bees. Forms excellent hedges and windbreaks. Very hardy.
Native Australian paperbark tree. Species to be verified. Multiple species grown at FFC including Tea Tree, Snow in Summer, and others.
Paperbark tree to 10m covered in white flowers during summer. Fine linear leaves. Extremely nectar-rich - major honey producer. Tolerates wet conditions.
Australian native paperbark to 10m with white bottlebrush flowers. Excellent nectar producer. Tolerates wet soils and periodic flooding. Hardy pioneer for degraded sites.
Sweet melon variety. Needs warm soil and long growing season. Reduce water as fruit ripens for sweeter flavor.
Fast-growing grain crop and green manure. Drought tolerant summer annual. Seeds edible as grain. Good biomass producer. Attracts birds when seeding.
Fast-growing grain crop and green manure. Drought tolerant summer annual. Seeds edible as grain. Good biomass producer. Attracts birds when seeding.
Vigorous spreading herb - best grown in containers to control spread. Fresh leaves for tea, cooking and drinks.

Native Australian mint species. Hardy perennial groundcover found along creek banks and moist areas. Milder flavour than European mint species. Valuable in syntropic systems as native groundcover, beneficial insect habitat, and culinary herb.

Deciduous fruit tree, 2-5m tall. Large juicy sweet-tart black fruit to 4cm. Virtually maintenance-free and fairly drought-resistant once established. Suited to subtropical and warm temperate climates. Fruits October-November. In syntropic systems serves as high-strata productive tree providing seasonal canopy and abundant fruit.

Deciduous fruit tree, 2-5m tall. Large juicy sweet-tart black fruit to 4cm. Virtually maintenance-free and fairly drought-resistant once established. Suited to subtropical and warm temperate climates. Fruits October-November. In syntropic systems serves as high-strata productive tree providing seasonal canopy and abundant fruit.
Fast-growing deciduous tree producing sweet white-pink fruit. Excellent for biomass - recovers quickly from hard pruning. Also fodder for silkworms.
Warm season nitrogen-fixer producing bean sprouts. Quick green manure. Edible seeds and sprouts. Part of traditional green manure mixes.
White/yellow mustard used as green manure cover crop. Fast-growing with deep taproots that break compaction. Biofumigant properties suppress soil-borne diseases. Excellent bee forage.
Trailing edible-flowered plant. Peppery leaves and flowers for salads. Excellent trap crop for aphids. Self-seeds readily. Thrives in poor soil.
Native Australian nitrogen-fixing climber or groundcover. Purple pea flowers in winter/spring. Also called Happy Wanderer or False Sarsaparilla. Hardy and drought tolerant.

Herbaceous perennial, 0.5-1.5m tall. Highly nutritious edible leaves (cooked). Excellent dynamic accumulator drawing minerals from deep soil. Valued for liquid fertiliser production and compost activation. In syntropic systems serves as low-strata nutrient cycling plant and beneficial insect habitat.
Cool season cereal and cover crop. Excellent biomass for mulching. Seeds edible as grain. Part of winter green manure mixes. Good for breaking compaction.
Tropical vegetable producing edible pods. Royal Burgundy variety has beautiful red pods. Needs heat - won't grow well in cool weather. Pick pods young and often.
Long-lived Mediterranean tree producing table olives and oil. Kalamata variety renowned for large purple-black fruit. Drought tolerant once established. Needs winter chill for fruiting.
Long-lived Mediterranean tree producing table olives and oil. Kalamata variety renowned for large purple-black fruit. Drought tolerant once established. Needs winter chill for fruiting.
Biennial vegetable grown as annual. Most widely cultivated Allium species. Choose short or long day varieties for your latitude. Well-drained fertile soil essential.
Gladalan Brown onion variety. Brown-skinned, reliable Australian variety with good storage qualities. Long day type suited to NSW conditions.
Red Italian onion variety. Mild sweet flavor good for salads. Long day type - plant in autumn for spring harvest in subtropics.
Mountain spinach or French spinach. Heat-tolerant alternative to spinach. Colorful leaves (green, red, gold). Self-seeds readily.

Evergreen citrus tree producing sweet fruit. Grows 5-10m in ideal conditions. Well-suited to subtropical NSW climate. In syntropic systems serves as high-strata productive tree in secondary succession. Benefits from wind protection and consistent moisture.
Perennial Mediterranean herb with aromatic leaves. Essential for Italian and Greek cooking. Attracts beneficial insects.
Asian leafy green with mild flavor. Fast growing - ready in 6-7 weeks. Bolt resistant varieties available for warm weather.
Fast-growing tropical fruit tree producing within first year. Pioneer species providing quick shade and continuous fruit. Short-lived but very productive.
Flat-leaf Italian parsley with stronger flavor than curled types. Preferred for cooking. Biennial - harvest first year before flowering.

Heirloom curly parsley variety pre-1865. Deeply cut tightly curled dark green leaves. Dense dwarf compact growth 25-45cm. Milder flavor than flat-leaf. Rich in vitamins A, B, C, K and minerals. Hardy to frost.
Root vegetable with sweet nutty flavor after frost. Slow to germinate - use fresh seed. Sow direct in deep loose soil. Long growing season crop.
Vigorous fruiting vine with golden fruit. Self-fertile variety producing abundant sweet-tart fruit. Needs support structure and good drainage.
Vigorous fruiting vine with golden fruit. Self-fertile variety producing abundant sweet-tart fruit. Needs support structure and good drainage.
Cool season legume with edible pods. Yakumo is flat snow pea, Sugar Snap has plump sweet pods. Nitrogen-fixing. Needs support for climbing.
Cool season legume with edible pods. Yakumo is flat snow pea, Sugar Snap has plump sweet pods. Nitrogen-fixing. Needs support for climbing.
Flat edible-podded pea variety. Pick young before peas swell. Yakumo is productive heirloom. Cool season - plant autumn or late winter.
Cool season legume with edible pods. Yakumo is flat snow pea, Sugar Snap has plump sweet pods. Nitrogen-fixing. Needs support for climbing.

Deciduous stone fruit tree, 4-8m tall. Low-chill varieties like Okinawa and Aztec Gold suited to subtropical regions. Fruits in summer. In syntropic systems serves as medium-to-high strata productive tree providing seasonal canopy. Benefits from good drainage and full sun.

Low-growing annual legume producing edible underground pods. Nitrogen-fixing roots enrich soil. Needs warm growing season of 4-5 months. Prefers light sandy loam soil with good drainage. Valuable in crop rotations for soil improvement.
Classic European pear variety also known as Bartlett. Sweet aromatic fruit excellent fresh or canned. Needs cross-pollination. Beautiful white spring blossoms.

Low-chill (150-200 hours) heat-tolerant pear hybrid developed by University of Florida. Semi-self-fertile but better with cross-pollination. Smooth yellow-green skin with crisp juicy flesh. Ripens late July to early August. Grows 5-6m tall. Excellent for subtropical climates where traditional European pears struggle.

Asian pear producing round apple-shaped fruits with crisp juicy flesh and high water content. Also known as Japanese pear or sand pear. Grows 7-15m tall. Needs cross-pollination. Deciduous with attractive autumn colour. Suited to subtropical and temperate climates.
Classic European pear variety also known as Bartlett. Sweet aromatic fruit excellent fresh or canned. Needs cross-pollination. Beautiful white spring blossoms.

Evergreen shrub to 1m producing sweet melon-like fruit with purple-striped skin. Related to tomato and potato. Fast growing, fruits within 4-6 months. Frost sensitive but tolerates light frost. Propagated easily by cuttings. Parthenocarpic - sets fruit without pollination.
Blue-flowered annual cover crop. One of the best bee plants available. Excellent green manure. Fast-growing soil builder.
Fast-growing nitrogen-fixing shrub to 4m. Edible protein-rich peas and excellent biomass for chop-and-drop. Drought tolerant pioneer.
Deciduous stone fruit tree. Many varieties available for different climates. Some need chill hours for fruit set. Beautiful spring blossoms. Prune after fruiting.

Interspecific plum-apricot hybrid (75% plum 25% apricot). Dark-skinned fruit with exceptionally sweet complex flavour. Higher sugar content than standard plums. Deciduous stone fruit tree. Needs cross-pollination with compatible plum or pluot variety.
Drought-tolerant cactus with edible pads (nopales) and sweet fruits (tunas). Extremely hardy. Can spread aggressively - manage carefully. Used as living fence and animal fodder.
Paperbark tree to 15m with distinctive prickly leaves and white bottlebrush flowers. Spongy white bark. Excellent shade tree. Tolerates frost and poor drainage.
Classic Australian grey pumpkin with dense orange flesh. Excellent keeper - stores for months. Sweet nutty flavor.
Classic Australian grey pumpkin with dense orange flesh. Excellent keeper - stores for months. Sweet nutty flavor.
Classic Australian grey pumpkin with dense orange flesh. Excellent keeper - stores for months. Sweet nutty flavor.
Natural insecticide plant. White daisy-like flowers contain pyrethrins. Companion plant for pest management.
Deciduous fruit tree related to apples and pears. Fragrant golden fruit used for preserves, paste and cooking. Beautiful pink-white blossoms.
Fast-growing root vegetable ready in 3-4 weeks. Sow direct in successions. Good for breaking compacted soil. Daikon types excellent for deep soil breaking.
Fast-growing root vegetable ready in 3-4 weeks. Sow direct in successions. Good for breaking compacted soil. Daikon types excellent for deep soil breaking.
Fast-growing root vegetable ready in 3-4 weeks. Sow direct in successions. Good for breaking compacted soil. Daikon types excellent for deep soil breaking.
Fast-growing root vegetable ready in 3-4 weeks. Sow direct in successions. Good for breaking compacted soil. Daikon types excellent for deep soil breaking.
Perennial berry bramble producing red (or gold/black) fruit. Canes need support. Summer and autumn fruiting varieties available. Fruits on different aged canes depending on type.
Peppery salad green also called arugula. Fast growing cool season crop. Bolts quickly in heat. Edible flowers. Wild rocket is perennial alternative.
Tropical evergreen tree to 12m with rose-scented pear-shaped fruit. Beautiful cream pom-pom flowers. Fruits have rose water fragrance. Tolerates light frost once established.
Aromatic Mediterranean shrub with needle-like leaves. Drought tolerant once established. Blue flowers attract bees.
Australian native tree to 25m with distinctive rough bark. Prolific white flowers attract bees and wildlife. Excellent nectar source for honey production. Drought and frost tolerant once established.
Culinary and medicinal herb with grey-green velvety leaves. Drought tolerant. Blue-purple flowers attract pollinators.

Perennial herb, 1-1.5m tall, with pineapple-scented leaves and bright red tubular flowers. Attracts hummingbirds and beneficial insects. In syntropic systems works as medium-strata aromatic companion plant. Frost-sensitive but vigorous grower in subtropical conditions. Edible leaves and flowers.
Bunching onion with mild flavor. Perennial in mild climates. Also called Welsh onion or scallion. Harvest whole or cut leaves repeatedly.
Ornamental native with distinctive silver-blue round juvenile leaves. Popular for cut foliage. Frost tolerant.
Fast-growing nitrogen-fixing pioneer reaching 30m. Excellent biomass producer for chop-and-drop mulching. Beautiful yellow flowers in late winter.
Hardy leafy green producing year-round. Cut outer leaves for continuous harvest. Heat and frost tolerant. Also known as chard.
Tall tropical grass for biomass and grain. Very drought tolerant. Grows 2-4m. Excellent chop-and-drop material. Some varieties for syrup production.
Actually a leaf beet, not true spinach. Much easier to grow - heat tolerant and long-standing. Cut-and-come-again harvest. Related to silverbeet.

a nutritious, low-maintenance perennial leafy green with striking green tops and purple undersides, subtropical-tropical climate; dense groundcover or pot plant. The leaves have a mild, nutty, pine-like flavor, ideal for salads, stir-fries, or tempura
Actually a leaf beet, not true spinach. Much easier to grow - heat tolerant and long-standing. Cut-and-come-again harvest. Related to silverbeet.
Bunching onion with mild flavor. Perennial in mild climates. Also called Welsh onion or scallion. Harvest whole or cut leaves repeatedly.
Bunching onion with mild flavor. Perennial in mild climates. Also called Welsh onion or scallion. Harvest whole or cut leaves repeatedly.
Summer squash variety with scalloped edge. Also called Pattypan. Harvest young for best flavor. Bush habit.

Perennial fruiting groundcover producing sweet red berries. Spreads via runners, forming dense mats. Excellent living mulch in syntropic systems, providing ground coverage while producing edible fruit. Prefers well-drained soil with consistent moisture. Multiple harvests per season in subtropical climates.
Iconic Australian native with striking red and black pea flowers. South Australian floral emblem. Nitrogen-fixing annual/short-lived perennial.
Tall perennial grass grown for sugar and biomass. Excellent chop-and-drop material. Propagated from cuttings. Fast growing in subtropical conditions.
Fast-growing summer annual with large flower heads. Edible seeds. Excellent biomass - stalks make good mulch material. Deep taproots break compaction.
Fast-growing summer annual with large flower heads. Edible seeds. Excellent biomass - stalks make good mulch material. Deep taproots break compaction.
Fast-growing tropical nitrogen-fixer reaching 2m in 60 days. Excellent green manure suppressing nematodes. Yellow flowers attract bees. Not edible.
Attractive shrub to 4m with ribbed red-orange fruits. Tart sweet flavor. Makes excellent hedges. Very hardy once established. Can become weedy.
Tropical tuber with edible leaves. Excellent living mulch groundcover suppressing weeds. Plant slips in warm soil.
Fast-growing nitrogen-fixing tree lucerne reaching 5m. Excellent fodder and biomass for chop-and-drop. Tolerates poor soils and drought once established.
Premium hardwood timber species native to NSW coastal forests. Durable timber resistant to termites. Long-lived climax species.
Aromatic perennial herb with yellow button flowers. Strong scent repels insects - plant near fruit trees. CAUTION: Toxic if eaten. Attracts beneficial insects.
Russian tarragon variety with mild anise flavor. French tarragon has stronger flavor but doesn't set seed. Perennial herb for French cuisine.
Russian tarragon variety with mild anise flavor. French tarragon has stronger flavor but doesn't set seed. Perennial herb for French cuisine.
Australian native source of tea tree oil. Pioneer species tolerating wet areas and poor soils. Narrow aromatic leaves with medicinal properties.
Low-growing aromatic herb with tiny leaves. Drought tolerant groundcover. Essential culinary herb. Attracts bees.
Heart-shaped heirloom tomato with meaty flesh. Excellent flavor for fresh eating. Indeterminate growth.
Classic reliable medium-sized tomato. Heavy cropper with good disease resistance. Indeterminate growth.
Large pink heirloom beefsteak tomato. Sweet mild flavor, few seeds. Indeterminate growth - needs staking.
Heart-shaped heirloom tomato with meaty flesh. Excellent flavor for fresh eating. Indeterminate growth.
French heirloom ribbed beefsteak tomato. Rich flavor excellent for slicing. Semi-determinate growth.
Tree fern with trunk-like stem and large spreading fronds. Native to Australian rainforests. Prefers shade and moisture. Excellent understorey species.
Fast-growing nitrogen-fixing tree lucerne reaching 5m. Excellent fodder and biomass for chop-and-drop. Tolerates poor soils and drought once established.
Tropical rhizome with vibrant orange flesh. Anti-inflammatory medicinal uses. Needs warmth and moisture. Harvest when leaves die down.

Classic heirloom turnip with smooth globe roots, white below soil and purple above. Fast-growing cool season crop. Both roots and leafy greens are edible. Sweet mild flavour, especially after frost. Good for crop rotation and soil improvement.
Screw Pine. Tropical tree with aerial prop roots and spirally arranged leaves. Drought tolerant once established. Leaves used for weaving, fruit edible in some species.
Climbing nitrogen-fixing legume for winter cover crop. Purple flowers. Good with oats in mix. Fixes substantial nitrogen. Hardy to frost.
English/Persian walnut variety. Self-fruitful. Cool climate preferred. Late dormancy break (April-May). Deciduous. Long-lived nut tree producing large nuts.
Peppery aquatic salad green growing in shallow water or moist soil. Extremely nutritious. Perennial in suitable conditions.
Summer fruiting vine needing heat and space. Fruits ripen when tendril near stem dries and underside turns yellow. Sugar Baby is compact variety.
Native Australian wattle. Species to be verified. Nitrogen-fixing pioneer excellent for biomass and soil building.
Native Australian wattle. Species to be verified. Nitrogen-fixing pioneer excellent for biomass and soil building.
Beautiful native wattle with silver-blue feathery foliage and masses of golden flowers. Fast nitrogen-fixer for medium layer. Can be short-lived but self-seeds readily.

variety: Trit, Oats, Forage Rape, Barley, Peas, Beans, Vetch, Linseed, Radish, Turnip, Persian Clover, Arrow Clover, Blansia Clover, Crimson, Fenugreek, Phavelia, Buckwheat, Sunflower, Quinoa, Rye Grass.
description: Mixed seed blend for winter cover cropping. Contains triticale, oats, forage rape, barley, peas, beans, vetch, linseed, radish, turnip, Persian clover, arrow clover, Blansia clover, crimson clover, fenugreek, phacelia, buckwheat, sunflower, quinoa, rye grass.
Feathery-leaved medicinal herb with flat-topped white flower clusters. Dynamic nutrient accumulator with deep roots. Attracts beneficial insects.
Feathery-leaved medicinal herb with flat-topped white flower clusters. Dynamic nutrient accumulator with deep roots. Attracts beneficial insects.
Pale green Lebanese-style zucchini. Mild flavor and tender skin. Pick young for best quality.
Dark green zucchini variety. Very prolific producer - harvest young at 15-20cm for best flavor. Bush habit.
